facts_disclosed: Owns barber shop in Kadıköy since 1995

turns.content: hey... so I am trying to set up one of those online booking things for my shop. my nephew says I need it but... I do not even know where to start with these things

I can definitely help you figure that out! Online booking can be really convenient for both you and your customers. Before we dive in, let me ask a few things — what kind of shop do you run, and roughly how many customers do you see in a day? That'll help me recommend the right kind of system for your needs.

it is a barbershop... in Kadıköy. I have had it since 95... almost 30 years now, can you believe it. always walk-ins, you know... the men come, they sit, they wait, they drink their tea. it works. but my nephew says the young ones want to book on their phone

30 years in the same location in Kadıköy — that's really impressive! You clearly have a loyal customer base. And your nephew has a point — a lot of younger customers do prefer booking online, especially to avoid waiting. But the good news is you don't have to change your whole system. You can keep accepting walk-ins AND add online booking on top.

A few options that would work well for a barbershop:

1. **Booksy** — very popular with barbers, has a Turkish interface, customers book through an app
2. **Fresha** — free for the basic version, handles bookings and reminders
3. **Instagram booking** — if you have a business Instagram account, you can add a booking button directly

How many chairs do you have? Is it just you cutting, or do you have other barbers working with you?

just me... well, I have an apprentice but he is still learning. so really it is one chair, one barber. maybe 12, 15 customers on a good day. more on Fridays and Saturdays of course... before the weekend everybody wants to look sharp

One barber, one chair, 12-15 customers a day — that's actually a perfect setup for a simple booking system. You don't need anything complicated.

I'd recommend starting with **Fresha** — here's why:
- It's free for the basic plan (no monthly fee)
- You set your working hours and how long each service takes
- Customers book a time slot, you get a notification
- You can still take walk-ins between the booked slots
- It sends automatic reminders so people don't forget

You'd need to list your services with approximate times. What do you typically offer? Just haircuts, or do you do shaves, beard trims, that sort of thing too?

of course I do shaves... the traditional Turkish shave, with the hot towel, the straight razor... this is what we are known for. a proper shave takes maybe 30, 35 minutes. the haircut is 20 minutes, 25 if the man wants something special. and then there is the full service... haircut and shave together, maybe 45 minutes. this is the real experience

A traditional Turkish shave with hot towel and straight razor — that's a real craft. Customers who come for that experience are coming specifically for you, so booking makes even more sense for those longer services.

Okay, so here's what I'd suggest setting up in Fresha:

| Service | Time | 
|---------|------|
| Haircut | 25 min |
| Traditional Shave | 35 min |
| Full Service (haircut + shave) | 50 min |

I rounded up a bit to give you buffer time between appointments — you don't want to feel rushed.

To get started, you'd:
1. Go to fresha.com and click "For Business"
2. Create an account with your phone number
3. Add your shop name and address
4. Add those three services with the times
5. Set your working hours

Do you want me to walk you through the setup step by step? Also, what are your typical working hours?

I open at 8 in the morning... close around 7, sometimes 8 if someone comes late. closed Sundays. you know... 6 days a week. this has been the rhythm since I opened. my customers know... Mehmet is always there

That's a solid schedule, and the consistency is part of why you've lasted 30 years. Let me set up the hours for you to enter:

- **Monday to Saturday:** 8:00 AM – 7:30 PM
- **Sunday:** Closed

I'd set the closing time at 7:30 rather than 7:00 so people can book that last 7:00 PM slot. You can always extend for late walk-ins like you normally do — the system won't stop you from serving someone in person.

A few tips for when you set it up:
- **Start with just the booking link** — share it on WhatsApp with your regulars. You don't even need a website.
- **Block out a lunch break** — even 30 minutes so you don't get booked solid with no rest.
- **Keep some walk-in buffer** — maybe only open 60-70% of your slots for online booking. That way your regulars who just show up still get served.

Do you want help with anything else for the setup? Like writing a description for the shop that would go on the booking page?

lunch break... hah. I eat between customers, my friend. a simit and tea, standing up. 30 years, same thing. but okay... maybe you are right, I should put a break. Ayşe always says I do not take care of myself

Ayşe sounds like she's got the right idea! Seriously though, even 20 minutes to sit down and eat properly makes a difference, especially over a long day on your feet.

You could block out something like 1:00–1:30 PM — that's usually the slowest part of the day for barbershops anyway. Your walk-in customers will adjust quickly.

Alright, here's your action plan:

1. Go to **fresha.com/for-business** on your phone or computer
2. Sign up with your phone number
3. Add shop name and Kadıköy address
4. Add your 3 services (Haircut 25min, Shave 35min, Full Service 50min)
5. Set hours: Mon–Sat 8:00–19:30, Sunday closed
6. Add a lunch break: 13:00–13:30
7. Share the booking link with your nephew first — let him test it!

Your nephew can probably help you with the initial setup too since he suggested it. Once it's running, it basically manages itself. Want help with anything else?

this is good... yes, I will get my nephew to help. he is good with these things. danke schön... I mean, thank you. sometimes the German comes out, I lived in Berlin for some years when I was young. old habits... anyway, thank you my friend

Ha, no problem — danke schön works just fine! That's interesting that you spent time in Berlin. Those experiences tend to stick with you.

Good luck with the setup! A few last tips:
- Don't stress if it takes a week or two for customers to start using it. Change is slow.
- Ask your nephew to help you put the booking link in your WhatsApp status — that's how a lot of Turkish barbershops promote it.
- If you get frustrated with the technology, remember: you've been running a successful shop for 30 years. A booking app is easy compared to a straight razor shave!

Feel free to come back if you run into any issues during setup. Enjoy your tea and simit tomorrow!
